2606.20946.title: Scaling Diverse Language Generation for 3D Visual Grounding
2606.20946.paper_summary: Developing robust models for 3D visual grounding (3DVG), the localization of entities in a 3D scene described in natural language, is important for enabling agents to correspond spatial language with objects in the physical world. However, the lack of diverse descriptions at scale prevents models from generalizing beyond simple linguistic patterns. Recent such attempts lack diversity in the constraint types and language used to ground objects. Captioning methods cannot precisely contrast objects, which is important for visual grounding. We therefore propose ViGiL3D++, a scalable, scene-agnostic method that generates diverse visual grounding queries by combining constraint sampling in scene graphs with the language generation of LLMs. We show that it has greater diversity over existing scaled datasets and improves model performance over several 3DVG benchmarks but also illuminates outstanding limitations of VLMs.
2606.20946.paper_summary_zh: 開發穩健的3D視覺定位模型（3DVG），即在自然語言中描述的3D場景中定位實體，對於使代理能夠將空間語言與物理世界中的物體相對應至關重要。<lb/>然而，缺乏多樣化的描述使得模型無法超越簡單的語言模式進行泛化。<lb/>最近的此類嘗試在約束類型和用於定位物體的語言上缺乏多樣性。<lb/>標題生成方法無法精確對比物體，而這對於視覺定位非常重要。<lb/>因此，我們提出了ViGiL3D++，這是一種可擴展的、場景無關的方法，通過將場景圖中的約束採樣與大型語言模型的語言生成相結合，生成多樣化的視覺定位查詢。<lb/>我們顯示它在現有的擴展數據集上具有更大的多樣性，並在幾個3DVG基準上提高了模型性能，但也揭示了VLMs的顯著限制。
2606.20946.author: Austin T. Wang et.al.
2606.20946.authors: Austin T. Wang, Dongchen Yang, Angel X. Chang
